verses.commentary: But mark this commands attention. There will be terrible times in the last days prophesies future hardship. Last days began at Christ's first coming and extend to his return. The entire church age is eschatological.

People will be lovers of themselves heads the vice list with root sin: self-love. Lovers of money adds greed. Boastful, proud, abusive, disobedient to their parents, ungrateful, unholy catalog moral collapse. The list moves from internal to relational sins.

Without love, unforgiving, slanderous, without self-control, brutal, not lovers of the good continues the catalog. Each vice represents absence of virtue. The portrait depicts comprehensive moral corruption.
Treacherous, rash, conceited adds three more. Lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God completes the list by contrasting wrong love with right. Misdirected love is the fundamental problem.

Having a form of godliness but denying its power describes religious hypocrisy. External religion without internal reality. Have nothing to do with such people commands separation. Avoid those who fake faith.

They are the kind who worm their way into homes describes deceptive infiltration. And gain control over gullible women targets vulnerable victims. Who are loaded down with sins and are swayed by all kinds of evil desires explains susceptibility. Guilt and instability create openness to exploitation.
Always learning but never able to come to a knowledge of the truth describes perpetual seeking without finding. Curiosity without conviction. Constant inquiry that never reaches conclusion.

Just as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses draws parallel to Exodus tradition of Pharaoh's magicians. So also these teachers oppose the truth applies pattern to present. They are men of depraved minds describes corrupt thinking. Who, as far as the faith is concerned, are rejected declares their disqualification.

But they will not get very far promises limited success. Because, as in the case of those men, their folly will be clear to everyone applies historical precedent. Error eventually exposes itself. Truth ultimately triumphs.

You, however, know all about my teaching contrasts Timothy with false teachers. My way of life, my purpose, faith, patience, love, endurance lists what Timothy observed. Paul's life authenticated his teaching.

Persecutions, sufferings recalls specific trials. What kinds of things happened to me in Antioch, Iconium and Lystra names locations. The persecutions I endured emphasizes suffering. Yet the Lord rescued me from all of them testifies to divine deliverance. Not immunity from suffering but preservation through it.
In fact, everyone who wants to live a godly life in Christ Jesus will be persecuted universalizes the principle. Godliness attracts hostility. Suffering is normal Christian experience, not exception.

While evildoers and impostors will go from bad to worse contrasts trajectory. Deceiving and being deceived describes double deception: they deceive others while being deceived themselves. Error compounds; sin accelerates.

But as for you, continue in what you have learned and have become convinced of commands perseverance. Because you know those from whom you learned it grounds confidence in trustworthy sources. Timothy's teachers validate his teaching.

And how from infancy you have known the Holy Scriptures recalls early exposure. Which are able to make you wise for salvation defines Scripture's purpose. Through faith in Christ Jesus identifies the lens. Scripture leads to Christ; Christ brings salvation.

All Scripture is God-breathed asserts divine inspiration. And is useful for teaching, rebuking, correcting and training in righteousness lists Scripture's four functions. Teaching provides knowledge, rebuking exposes error, correcting realigns conduct, training develops maturity.
So that the servant of God may be thoroughly equipped for every good work states Scripture's goal. Complete equipment for complete obedience. The word produces the worker.
